pi_items.source: Verified in setup_task.sh which copies the selected sample image to this exact path.
Verified in setup_task.sh and export_result.sh, both of which explicitly define and use this directory.
Verified in verifier.py, which awards points for "Render successful" if "new_files >= 20".
Verified in verifier.py, which tests for Ease-In (position at 25% time < 0.20) and Ease-Out (position at 75% time > 0.80), forming a sigmoid position curve.

privileged_info_summary: The task generates a synthetic animation using a source asset from the official OpenToonz sample repository. It was verified that the initial asset path is /home/ga/Desktop/asset.tif and the expected output directory for PNG frames is /home/ga/OpenToonz/output/ease_test. The verifier.py logic explicitly requires a minimum of 20 rendered frames. The expected "Sigmoid velocity curve (slow-fast-slow)" is verified by checking if the horizontal position is < 20% at the 25% time mark (Ease In), and > 80% at the 75% time mark (Ease Out). Note: setup_task.sh attempts to use "dwanko_run.0001.tif", which does not exist in the official repo; it falls back to the first available image, which is often a large background image (e.g., "01_sky.tif") instead of a character.
